description_long: This object is a compact, handheld electronic game controller with ergonomic, rounded grips on both sides for comfortable handling. The central section is slightly recessed and flanked by two detachable modules, each featuring a set of buttons and analog sticks. The left module has a directional pad and several small circular buttons, while the right module includes a larger analog stick and additional buttons, likely for action or menu navigation. The surface is smooth and appears to be made of molded plastic, with subtle seams indicating the modular construction. The overall design is symmetrical and streamlined, suggesting it is intended for portable use and easy handling. The object is likely inspired by or compatible with modern gaming consoles, such as the Nintendo Switch.
description: A compact, ergonomic handheld game controller with detachable side modules, analog sticks, and multiple buttons. The smooth, molded plastic body is designed for comfortable grip and portable use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both ergonomic grips, the central recessed section, and the symmetrical layout of buttons and analog sticks.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the rounded grip and the profile of the analog stick and buttons on the right module.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the controller, the full width of the grips, and the central seam between the modules.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the opposite side, showing the left grip, directional pad, and button arrangement.
